annotations.label: Maximal ideal (lead)

anchor.snippet: a maximal ideal is an ideal that is maximal

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsMaximal` defines a maximal ideal as a coatom in the lattice of (left/two-sided) ideals.

annotations.label: Quotient by maximal ideal is simple/field

anchor.snippet: the quotients of rings by maximal ideals are simple rings

annotations.note: `Ideal.Quotient.field` constructs a `Field` structure on `R ⧸ I` when `I.IsMaximal` for commutative `R`.

annotations.label: Maximal spectrum

anchor.snippet: The set of maximal ideals of a unital commutative ring

annotations.note: `MaximalSpectrum R` is the type of maximal ideals of a commutative ring `R`.

annotations.label: Maximal one-sided ideals

anchor.snippet: a maximal right ideal is defined analogously as being a maximal element in the poset of proper right ideals

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Ideal` models left ideals (with two-sided in commutative case); right ideals are not a separate first-class concept, so maximal right ideals are not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Local ring via unique maximal right ideal

anchor.snippet: If R has a unique maximal right ideal, then R is known as a local ring

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing` is defined via the `a+b=1 → IsUnit a ∨ IsUnit b` condition; equivalence with a unique maximal ideal is `IsLocalRing.maximal_ideal_unique`, but only for (commutative-style) two-sided ideals.

annotations.label: 2x2 matrices: unique two-sided but many one-sided maximal

anchor.snippet: in the ring of 2 by 2 square matrices over a field, the zero ideal is a maximal two-sided ideal

annotations.note: No specific Mathlib statement about maximal ideals of `Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) K` was found.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for maximal two-sided ideal

anchor.snippet: I is a maximal ideal of R if any of the following equivalent conditions hold

annotations.note: `Ideal.isMaximal_iff` gives one characterization (proper + every strictly larger ideal contains 1); the full list of equivalences from the article is not packaged as one decl.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for maximal right ideal

anchor.snippet: the following conditions are equivalent to A being a maximal right ideal of R

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a dedicated right-ideal API, so this list of equivalences is not formalized.

annotations.label: Field has only {0} as maximal ideal

anchor.snippet: If F is a field, then the only maximal ideal is

annotations.note: `Ideal.bot_isMaximal` proves `⊥` is maximal in a division (semi)ring, which together with `eq_bot_or_top` for ideals of a field captures the example.

annotations.label: Maximal ideals of Z

anchor.snippet: In the ring Z of integers, the maximal ideals are the principal ideals generated by a prime number

annotations.note: `Int.ideal_span_isMaximal_of_prime` shows the ideal `(p)` is maximal in ℤ when `p` is a prime number.

annotations.label: Nonzero primes maximal in PID

anchor.snippet: all nonzero prime ideals are maximal in a principal ideal domain

annotations.note: `PrincipalIdealRing.isMaximal_of_irreducible` together with `Ideal.IsPrime.isMaximal` (under `DimensionLEOne`) gives the result; PIDs are zero/one-dimensional so nonzero primes are maximal.

annotations.label: Maximal ideals of Z[x]

anchor.snippet: Generally, the maximal ideals of

annotations.note: No explicit Mathlib characterization of the maximal ideals of `ℤ[X]` was found.

annotations.label: Boolean ring: every prime is maximal

anchor.snippet: Every prime ideal is a maximal ideal in a Boolean ring

annotations.note: Mathlib's `BooleanRing` file has no theorem identifying prime and maximal ideals.

annotations.label: Maximal ideals of k[x]

anchor.snippet: The maximal ideals of the polynomial ring

annotations.note: `PrincipalIdealRing.isMaximal_of_irreducible` applied to `k[X]` (which is a PID over a field) gives that ideals generated by irreducible polynomials are maximal.

annotations.label: Weak Nullstellensatz

anchor.snippet: This result is known as the weak Nullstellensatz

annotations.note: `MvPolynomial.isMaximal_iff_eq_vanishingIdeal_singleton` characterizes maximal ideals of `k[x₁,…,xₙ]` over an algebraically closed field as vanishing ideals of points.

annotations.label: Maximal ideals of coordinate ring

anchor.snippet: the maximal ideals of the coordinate ring

annotations.note: No direct Mathlib statement was found describing maximal ideals of the coordinate ring of an affine variety.

annotations.label: R/m field iff m maximal

anchor.snippet: k = R / m is a field if and only if m is a maximal ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.Quotient.maximal_ideal_iff_isField_quotient` states `I.IsMaximal ↔ IsField (R ⧸ I)` for commutative `R`.

annotations.label: Residue field

anchor.snippet: R / m is known as the residue field

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing.ResidueField R` is defined as `R ⧸ maximalIdeal R` with a `Field` instance, covering the local-ring case of the residue field.

annotations.label: Simple modules arise from maximal left ideals

anchor.snippet: If L is a maximal left ideal, then R / L is a simple left

annotations.note: `isSimpleModule_iff_quot_maximal` shows `IsSimpleModule R M ↔ ∃ I : Ideal R, I.IsMaximal ∧ Nonempty (M ≃ₗ[R] R ⧸ I)`.

annotations.label: Krull's theorem

anchor.snippet: Krull's theorem (1929): Every nonzero unital ring has a maximal ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.exists_maximal` proves every nontrivial (semi)ring has a maximal ideal, with `Ideal.exists_le_maximal` giving the stronger relative form.

annotations.label: Radical ring has no maximal one-sided ideals

anchor.snippet: A radical ring , i.e. a ring in which the Jacobson radical is the entire ring

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl about radical rings (Jacobson radical = whole ring) and absence of one-sided maximal ideals was found.

annotations.label: Maximal implies prime in commutative ring

anchor.snippet: In a commutative ring with unity, every maximal ideal is a prime ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsMaximal.isPrime` (with an `instance IsMaximal.isPrime'`) gives that maximal ideals of a commutative (semi)ring are prime.

annotations.label: Zero-dimensional ring

anchor.snippet: Commutative rings in which prime ideals are maximal are known as zero-dimensional rings

annotations.note: `Ring.KrullDimLE 0 R` expresses that the Krull dimension of `R` is at most zero, i.e., every prime ideal is maximal.

annotations.label: Preimage under k-algebra homomorphism

anchor.snippet: the preimage of a maximal ideal of a finitely generated

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Jacobson-ring machinery (`Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing`, `MvPolynomial.comp_C_integral_of_surjective_of_isJacobsonRing`) from which this preimage statement follows, but no direct lemma `(comap of maximal under finitely generated k-algebra hom is maximal)`.

annotations.label: Maximal ideal of noncommutative ring not prime

anchor.snippet: A maximal ideal of a noncommutative ring might not be prime in the commutative sense

annotations.note: No Mathlib example of a noncommutative maximal ideal failing the commutative-prime condition was found.

annotations.label: Maximal submodule

anchor.snippet: a maximal submodule M of A is a submodule

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses 'maximal submodule' as `IsCoatom (N : Submodule R M)` using the general order-theoretic `IsCoatom`; there is no dedicated `Submodule.IsMaximal`.

annotations.label: Maximal right ideals = maximal submodules of R_R

anchor.snippet: The maximal right ideals of a ring R are exactly the maximal submodules of the module

annotations.note: `Ideal.isMaximal_def` (`I.IsMaximal ↔ IsCoatom I`) gives this identification for left ideals (Mathlib's `Ideal R = Submodule R R`); the right-ideal version is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of maximal submodules

anchor.snippet: finitely generated nonzero modules have maximal submodules, and also projective modules have maximal submodules

annotations.note: `Module.Finite.instIsCoatomicSubmodule` gives `IsCoatomic (Submodule R M)` for finitely generated `M`; the projective-module case is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maximal sub-bimodule

anchor.snippet: maximal ideals can be generalized by defining a maximal sub-bimodule

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated notion of a maximal sub-bimodule.
